content: Employees receive 20 days of paid annual leave per year. Up to 5 unused days can be carried over to the next year. Leave must be requested at least 2 weeks in advance for periods longer than 3 days.
Employees receive 10 days of paid sick leave per year. A doctor's note is required for absences longer than 3 consecutive days. Unused sick days do not carry over.
Primary caregivers receive 16 weeks of paid parental leave. Secondary caregivers receive 4 weeks. Leave must begin within 12 months of the birth or adoption.
Software subscriptions under $75/month can be expensed directly with receipt. Annual subscriptions over $500 require manager pre-approval. All software must be on the approved vendor list.
Hardware purchases over $500 require manager approval. Laptops and monitors are provided by IT. Home office equipment has a one-time $1,500 allowance for new employees.
Domestic flights must be booked economy class. Hotel stays are capped at $200/night. Meal expenses are reimbursed up to $60/day. All travel requires pre-approval from your manager.
Team dinners and events are capped at $75 per person. The team lead must submit the expense with an attendee list. Events over $500 total require director approval.
Employees may work remotely up to 3 days per week. Core hours are 10:00 AM to 3:00 PM in your local time zone. All remote workers must be reachable via Slack during core hours.
Core hours are 10:00 AM to 3:00 PM local time. Meetings should be scheduled within core hours. Flexible start/end times are allowed outside core hours with manager agreement.

All code changes must go through pull requests. PRs require at least one approval from a team member. The main branch is protected — no direct pushes. Use feature branches named feature/TICKET-description.
Python code must pass ruff linting and black formatting. All public functions require docstrings. Test coverage must be at least 80% for new code. Type hints are required for function signatures.
New employees receive a laptop on day 1. IT setup takes about 2 hours. Schedule 1-on-1s with your manager and team lead in the first week. Complete security training by end of week 1.
Complete all compliance training modules by end of month 1. Shadow at least 2 team members on their current projects. Submit your 30-day reflection document to your manager.

Report security incidents to security@acmecorp.com immediately. Do not attempt to investigate on your own. All incidents must be reported within 24 hours of discovery. The security team will triage and respond.
Passwords must be at least 16 characters. Use a password manager (1Password is provided). Enable 2FA on all company accounts. Never share credentials via Slack or email.
Backend: Python 3.12, FastAPI, PostgreSQL. Frontend: React, TypeScript, Next.js. Infrastructure: AWS, Docker, Kubernetes. CI/CD: GitHub Actions. Monitoring: Datadog.
For IT issues, submit a ticket at help.acmecorp.com or message #it-support on Slack. Emergency issues (account lockout, security breach) can be escalated by calling the IT hotline at ext. 5555.
Acme Corp provides medical, dental, and vision insurance. Coverage begins on your start date. The company covers 80% of premiums for employees and 50% for dependents. Open enrollment is in November.
Performance reviews happen twice a year in June and December. Self-assessments are due 2 weeks before the review. Ratings use a 1-5 scale. Promotions are discussed during the December cycle.
All meetings must have an agenda shared at least 1 hour before. Meetings should default to 25 or 50 minutes (not 30 or 60). No-agenda meetings can be declined. Fridays are meeting-free.
